channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NaVIC Navigation System Faces Operational Challenges with Only Three Satellites Active
item.description: India’s regional navigation system NaVIC, developed at a cost exceeding Rs 3,200 crore, is currently operational with only three functional satellites. NaVIC was designed to function optimally with a ...

item.title: Italian Fans Reject Idea of Replacing Iran in World Cup
item.description: A proposal made by Donald Trump's special envoy, Paolo Zampolli, to replace Iran with Italy at the 2026 World Cup has drawn strong reactions from Italian football fans. While Zampolli, an Italian-Amer...

item.title: More Than 50 Women Rescued from Human Trafficking in Pasig KTV Club Raid
item.description: In a recent operation by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), more than 50 women were rescued from a KTV club in Pasig City that was allegedly involved in sex trafficking activities. Authoritie...

item.title: Only One Applicant Approved Under Trump’s $1 Million Gold Card Visa Scheme
item.description: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick don confirm say na only one person don successfully get approval under President Donald Trump ‘gold card’ visa programme since dem launch am. This special residenc...

item.title: Service dogs recognised at national awards for military and healthcare contributions
item.description: Service animals that have played critical roles in both military operations and civilian care were recognised at the 2026 Australian Animal Distinguished Service Awards, held in Canberra at the Austra...

item.title: Adhir Chowdhury accuses Trinamool of influencing women's voter turnout
item.description: Adhir Chowdhury, leader of the Congress party, has alleged that the Trinamool Congress (TMC) resorted to enticement and intimidation to influence women's voter turnout during the recent elections. Acc...

item.title: CPI(M) adopts Mamdani-inspired social media style for West Bengal elections
item.description: The Communist Party of India (Marxist) is turning to social media with a fresh approach inspired by Zohran Mamdani’s viral campaign in New York, as it tries to regain relevance in West Bengal politics...

item.title: Chiefs trade up for cornerback while Browns add picks and select offensive tackle in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: The Cleveland Browns and Kansas City Chiefs completed the first trade of the first round in the 2026 NFL Draft, with both teams addressing key roster needs through the deal. Cleveland traded the No. 6...

item.title: Nike announces workforce reduction amid ongoing sales decline and restructuring efforts
item.description: Nike has announced plans to lay off approximately 1,400 employees as part of a broader effort to streamline operations and address a prolonged decline in sales. According to an internal memo from Chie...

item.title: Improved fitness and form boost Kaizer Chiefs ahead of Soweto derby clash with Pirates
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs head into the upcoming Soweto derby against Orlando Pirates in a far stronger position, according to co-coach Khalil Ben Youssef. After a difficult spell in February marked by multiple i...

item.title: Photographer Captures Intimate Moments With a Humpback Whale Before Its Tragic Death
item.description: Underwater photographer Rachel Moore experienced a rare and profound encounter with a female humpback whale named Sweet Girl. During the encounter, Sweet Girl displayed remarkable curiosity and calmne...

item.title: Coalition faces tough battle to retain Farrer seat after Sussan Ley’s resignation
item.description: The article examines the shifting political dynamics in the New South Wales seat of Farrer following the resignation of long-serving Liberal MP Sussan Ley. Once considered a safe Liberal stronghold fo...

item.title: Religious institutions face challenges in engaging single adults as traditional family-focused models decline
item.description: Religious communities in the United States and beyond are grappling with significant demographic shifts as the proportion of single adults rises. Historically, churches, synagogues, and mosques have s...

item.title: Chief Justice Afridi raises concerns over transfer of IHC judges
item.description: Islamabad – Chief Justice of Pakistan, Yahya Afridi, has raised serious concerns regarding the upcoming Judicial Commission of Pakistan (JCP) meeting, scheduled for April 28, where the transfer of fiv...

item.title: Understanding the Practical Challenges of AI in Africa
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has generated a lot of hype over the last few years, often portrayed as a technology that is already reshaping the world. However, the reality is far more practical and gr...

item.title: Trump Hosts Key Talks on Israel–Lebanon Ceasefire as Tensions with Hezbollah Escalate
item.description: US President Donald Trump is hosting talks at the White House aimed at extending a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. This follows escalating violence, particularly Israeli airstrikes in southern...

item.title: James Valentine’s Voluntary Assisted Dying: Barriers to Access Across Australia
item.description: James Valentine, the beloved broadcaster and saxophonist, passed away this week on his own terms, opting for voluntary assisted dying (VAD) in his final days. He made the decision to use VAD after bei...

item.title: Cardoso demands quick bounce-back from Sundowns after Stellenbosch draw
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns coach Miguel Cardoso has called for an immediate reaction from his side after they dropped points for the first time this year, settling for a 1-1 draw against Stellenbosch at Loftus...

item.title: NDLEA raises concern over rising drug abuse as 14 million Nigerians affected
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don raise serious alarm say more than 14 million Nigerians dey affected by illicit drug use, with about 400,000 people from Ondo State among them. The ...

item.title: RFK Jr. echoes Trump’s inaccurate claims about drug price reductions and misinterprets percentage calculations
item.description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. publicly defended President Donald Trump’s claims about dramatically reducing prescription drug prices, despite widespread criticism that thos...
